place: Burleigh CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Burleigh CDP, originally called Whitesboro, was founded in 1901 by the Equitable Industrial Association, a group of prominent Black investors including Paul Laurence Dunbar and Booker T. Washington.
The community was named for George Henry White, a Washington lawyer and former congressman who led the investment effort that created the town.
In 2006 the Johnson Family Historical Trust discovered the original deed to Whitesboro, confirming its status as one of “America’s Untold Treasures.”
This summer a dig for national history will take place in Whitesboro, hosted by the Johnson Family Historical Trust, to uncover more artifacts from the town’s founding investors.
Burleigh’s Martin Luther King Jr. Community Center, located in Whitesboro, serves as a hub for civil‑rights heritage and community events.
Stedman Graham, a former high‑school basketball star, was born in Whitesboro and later became an educator, author, and partner of Oprah Winfrey.
